titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Leonor Fini Papers
titlestmt.author: Sandrine Guérin

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0, has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number:

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday April 29, 2026 at 23:32

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.physdesc: 54 boxes (Series I. Correspondence and collected material)
did.abstract: The Leonor Fini papers document the life and career of painter, theater and costume designer, illustrator, and author Leonor Fini. The collection includes correspondence, writings of Leonor Fini, writings of others, photographs and scrapbooks, artwork and art documentation, costume and set design visual documentation and performance programs, clippings, business records, and audio materials. Also present are papers and related materials by or concerning her longtime companions Stanislao Lepri and Konstanty Jeleński. The collection spans the 1910s to 2016, with the bulk of the material dating from the 1930s to 1995.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Series I - Correspondence and Collected Materials is open for research. Additional series are closed until processed.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Glenn Horowitz Bookseller, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2019, 2021, and from Librairie Benoît Forgeot, on the Edith and Richard French Fund, 2021, 2024.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I. Correspondence and collected materials, 1929-2016 (bulk 1930s-1995).

bioghist.head: Leonor Fini (1907-1996)
bioghist.p: Leonor Fini (Buenos Aires, Argentina, August 30, 1907–Aubervilliers, France, January 18, 1996) was an Italian painter, designer, illustrator, and author.

Leonor Fini was born on August 30, 1907, in Buenos Aires to Malvina Braun Dubich (Trieste, 1884?–1971) and Hermino Fini (Argentina, c. 1875–?). After her parents separated during her infancy, she moved with her mother to Trieste—then part of the Austro-Hungarian Empire—where she was raised in a cosmopolitan environment. Her uncle, Ernesto Braun, was closely connected to the Triestine intelligentsia and frequent visitors to the household included Roberto Bazlen, James Joyce, Umberto Saba, and Italo Svevo. Fini began painting at an early age and Arturo Nathan became an influential mentor, providing lessons, engaging in discussions about art, and sharing his library of art books.

In 1928, Fini studied in Milan with Achille Funi and became associated with the Novecento group (including Funi, Mario Sironi, and Piero Marussig) as well as artists linked to Metaphysical painting, such as Carlo Carrà, Giorgio de Chirico, and Alberto Savinio. She moved to Paris in the fall of 1931 where she encountered a wide circle of artists and intellectuals connected to Surrealist and avant-garde movements. During that period she formed personal and professional relationships with Eugene Berman, Victor Brauner, Paul Éluard, Max Ernst, Meret Oppenheim, André Pieyre de Mandiargues, and Pierre Roy, among others. Between 1933 and 1935, her work appeared in several exhibitions, including La Peinture italienne contemporaine at the Musée du Luxembourg; Comme ils se voient, comme ils sont, at Galerie Jacques Bonjean; Dessins Surréalistes, at Galerie des Quatre chemins.

In 1936, Fini exhibited with Max Ernst at Julien Levy's gallery in New York and participated in the Museum of Modern Art's landmark exhibition Fantastic Art, Dada, and Surrealism. Although associated with Surrealist circles, she did not formally affiliate with the movement and openly criticized André Breton, its leading figure, for what she viewed as his misogyny and dogmatism.

At the outbreak of World War II, Fini left Paris with her companions André Pieyre de Mandiargues and Federico Veneziani, traveling first to Arcachon—where they joined Salvador Dalí and Gala—and later settling in Monte Carlo in the summer of 1940. There she continued to paint, supporting herself in part through portrait commissions for friends, wealthy patrons, and members of the European aristocracy from France, England, Poland, Italy, and Russia. In January 1941, she met Stanislao Lepri, then Italian consul in Monte Carlo. When he was recalled to Italy in 1943, they moved together to Rome, where they associated with artists and intellectuals. Fini exhibited at Galleria La Margherita in January 1945, along with Fabrizio Clerici and Alberto Savinio. The gallery, directed by Irene Brin and Gaspero del Corso, served as an important artistic and cultural hub in Rome.

Fini and Lepri returned to Paris in 1946. Her work continued to explore the dynamics of gender and identity, often through mythological and symbolic imagery. In January 1952, she met Konstanty Jeleński (known as "Kot") in Rome; later that year he joined Fini and Lepri in Paris, where the three would live together permanently.

After World War II, Fini expanded her practice to include set and costume design for ballet, theater, and film. She designed for the Paris Opera; George Balanchine's Palais de Cristal (1947, later Symphony in C); and Roland Petit's Les Demoiselles de la Nuit (1948), as well as productions featuring Maria Callas at La Scala in Milan. Between 1946 and 1969, she contributed designs to more than seventy theatrical productions in Paris, including works by Jean Anouilh, Jacques Audiberti, and Jean Genet. She also created costumes for films such as Renato Castellani's Romeo and Juliet (1954), Federico Fellini's 8½ (1963), and John Huston's A Walk with Love and Death (1969).

In addition, she illustrated approximately fifty books by authors including Charles Baudelaire, Jean Genet, Paul Verlaine, Gérard de Nerval, and Edgar Allan Poe; among her best-known contributions are her illustrations for a 1944 edition of the Marquis de Sade's Juliette. In the 1970s, Fini published three novels: Mourmour, conte pour enfants velus (1976), L'Onéiropompe (1978), and Rogomelec (1979). Fini continued working into the 1990s, producing paintings until two years before her death. During her lifetime, she was the subject of retrospective exhibitions in Belgium (1965), Italy (1983), Japan (1972–73, 1985–86), and France (1986).

Throughout her life, Leonor Fini resisted artistic and social categorization, rejecting conventional notions of gender roles and identity. She maintained a lifelong partnership with Stanislao Lepri (1905–1980) and Konstanty Jeleński (1922–1987), forming a singular domestic and creative arrangement. She also maintained a close circle of collaborators and friends with whom she shared artistic and intellectual pursuits, and some of these relationships ended in decisive ruptures. From 1954 to 1980, she rented a former Franciscan monastery in Nonza, Corsica, where she resided annually between June and September. There, she, Stanislao, and Kot devoted themselves to painting and writing and, with close friends, created elaborate ceremonies that blended art and performance.
Leonor Fini is buried in the cemetery of Saint-Dyé-sur-Loire, France, beside Stanislao Lepri and Konstanty "Kot" Jeleński.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Leonor Fini organized her papers and retained responsibility for the attribution and authentication of her works during her lifetime. Following her death in 1996, custodial and authentication responsibilities were assumed by Rafael Martinez, Fini's personal and administrative assistant since 1969 and her literary executor. After Martinez's death in 1998, custodial responsibility for the Leonor Fini Papers and administrative responsibility for the Leonor Fini Archives (8, rue de la Vrillière, Paris) were assumed by Richard Overstreet (1935–), estate representative and archive director. From 1998 to 2021, Overstreet controlled access to the papers and supported editorial and curatorial activities, including projects related to Konstanty Jeleński and Stanislao Lepri. Records created by Overstreet in the course of these activities remain in the collection as evidence of custodial management.

In 2019, custodial responsibility for a portion of the Leonor Fini Papers held by the estate was transferred to the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, with the remaining materials transferred in 2021. Smaller additions were acquired separately from other sources in 2021 and 2024. It includes a group of select letters that had been sold by the Fini estate in 2008 to the firm Aristophil/Musée des Manuscrits, which subsequently liquidated its assets following fraud charges and sold the literary material at auctions. And in March 2024, letters from Leonor Fini to art critic Castor Seibel from the collection of André Guichard were sold at auction by Christie's, and acquired by the Library. Provenance of material is captured in the descriptive notes.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Leonor Fini Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Processing is ongoing. Access will be provided in stages beginning in March 2026, with Series I. Correspondence and collected materials. The finding aid will be updated as additional series are opened.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Leonor Fini papers document the life and career of painter, theater and costume designer, illustrator, and author Leonor Fini. The collection includes correspondence, writings of Leonor Fini, writings of others, photographs and scrapbooks, artwork and art documentation, costume and set design visual documentation and performance programs, clippings, business records, and audio materials. Also present are papers and related materials by or concerning her longtime companions Stanislao Lepri and Konstanty Jeleński. The collection spans the 1910s to 2016, with the bulk of the material dating from the 1930s to 1995.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Leonor Fini Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence is chiefly in French and Italian, with additional materials in English, German, Polish, and Spanish.

The series documents Leonor Fini's personal and professional relationships and activities as an artist, set and costume designer, illustrator, and author from the 1930s through her death in 1996; with limited post-1996 correspondence from her literary heirs.

The series consists primarily of incoming correspondence, with outgoing correspondence identified at the file level. Outgoing letters are filed after incoming correspondence within individual correspondent files. Additional draft letters by Fini with unidentified recipients are located in "Draft letters to friends and acquaintances" within the subseries Other correspondence and collected materials.

The correspondence addresses artistic production, cultural events, daily life, health concerns, professional and personal relationships, and it includes drafts, annotated letters, and occasional enclosures such as drawings. The series documents key periods in Fini's life, including her early years in Milan and arrival in Paris (1931–1939), wartime residences in Monaco (1940–1942) and Rome (1943–1946), postwar decades (1946–1960s), and her later career. It reflects her relationships with a broad network of artists, writers, critics, intellectuals, collectors, family members, collaborators, and professional associates.

Early correspondence from the 1930s and 1940s documents Fini's Italian artistic circle, her integration into the Parisian artistic and literary milieu, her movements during the Second World War, and her return to Paris in 1946. Correspondents include artists and writers such as Eugène Berman, Leonora Carrington, Corrado Cagli, Jean Cocteau, Salvador Dalí and Gala, Paul Éluard, Max Ernst, Charles Henri Ford, Achille Funi, Henri Héraut, Georges Hugnet, Dora Maar, René Magritte, Elsa Morante, Arturo Nathan, Meret Oppenheim (7 folders), Giuseppe "Pippo" Rizzo, Pierre Roy, Pavel Tchelitchew (7 folders), Arturo Tosi, Antonio "Tono" Zancanaro, and Hans Bellmer and Unica Zürn. Some letters document Fini's wartime movements and relationships, including letters from André Pieyre de Mandiargues, Leonora Carrington, Elena Cassin, Léon Kochnitzky, and Jean Fraysse.

The series also includes correspondence reflecting Fini's involvement in opera, ballet, scenography and interior design, with figures such as Boris Kochno, Nicholas Kopeikine ("Kolya"), Dario Cecchi, Georges de Cuevas, Tao Izzo, Pierre Lacotte, Raymundo de Larrain, and Valerian Stux-Rybar.

Correspondence with writers, critics, and intellectuals includes Jacques Audiberti (3 folders), Claude Aveline, Héctor Bianciotti, Roberto "Bobi" Bazlen, Pierre Borgue, Marcel Brion, Michel Dard, Jean-Claude Dedieu (2 folders), Yves Florenne, Xavière Gauthier, Jean Genet, Claude Louis-Combet and Mireille Gerschwiler, Claire Goll, Edmond Jaloux, Marcel Jouhandeau, Théo Lesoualc'h, Francis de Miomandre, Alberto Moravia, Gloria Orenstein, Mario Praz, Wolfgang Sauré, Castor Seibel (11 folders incoming, 20 folders outgoing), Claude Serbanne, and Sherban Sidéry.

The series documents personal relationships and long-standing friendships, including partners André Pieyre de Mandiargues (2 boxes), Federico Veneziani (7 folders), and Sforzino Sforza (3 folders), as well as romantic relationships with Adolfo (unidentified), Reynold Arnould, Roland Bouvier (4 folders), and Paul Henri (6 folders). Correspondence with friends includes exchanges with Gerda Brentani (including letters with original drawings), Geneviève Chauvière ("Vette"), Fabrizio Clerici (5 folders), Enrico Colombotto Rosso (6 folders), Felicita Frai ("Mimi"), Francesco Grandjacquet (4 folders), Henri Guigonnat, Yvonne Larsen, Marianne Leibl (4 folders; includes horoscopes and graphological analyses), Ernst and Karin van Leyden (2 folders), Anna Magnani, Richard Overstreet (7 boxes incoming, 13 boxes outgoing), Cesare Pavani (2 folders), Juan Bautista Piñeiro (2 folders), Fred Deux and Cécile Reims (4 folders), Henri Rumeau (3 folders), Margherita Russo, Siné and Anick Sinet, Dorothea Tanning, Pao Ying Wang (2 folders), and Renato Wild. Family correspondence includes letters from her mother, Malvina Braun (5 boxes incoming, 4 boxes outgoing), her uncle, Ernesto Braun, and her cousins, Mary Frausin and Oscar de Mejo (3 folders).

Additional letters from an international network of artists, writers, collectors, and acquaintances include Marcelo Anchorena, Victor Brauner, Cecil Beaton, Alice Ceresa, Edulji Dinshaw, Federico Fellini, Suzanne Flon, Edward James, Gabrielle Dorziat, Alberto Giacometti, Irène Hamoir, Raoul Leven, Peter Lindamood, Joyce Mansour, Gabriel Pomerand, Suzy Solidor, Jacques Wergifosse and others.

The volume of correspondence ranges from single letters or postcards to exchanges spanning several decades. All identified correspondents have been assigned individual files to facilitate access; the extent of correspondence for each correspondent is indicated at the file level.

In some cases, correspondence includes annotated letters, manuscripts, drawings, or other enclosures. Enclosures have been retained with their original correspondence when possible. Materials that could not be confidently associated, as well as loose autograph notes, typescripts, and related items, are filed in Other correspondence and collected materials.

The correspondence arrived in varying levels of organization; a substantial portion was unsorted, with many correspondents unidentified and letters undated. To facilitate access, dates and attributions have been supplied where possible.

Additional correspondence, including professional files and materials by or relating to Konstanty Jeleński and Stanislao Lepri, will be located in Series. Professional files , Series. Konstanty "Kot" Jeleński papers, and in the separate collection of Stanislao Lepri papers (opening in 2026).
Series I. Correspondence and collected materials is organized in two subseries:
• General Correspondence, arranged alphabetically. Correspondence identified by first name only is filed at the end of the subseries under "First name only."
• Other correspondence and collected materials, arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five autograph letters, signed, two autograph postcards, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, two autograph postcards, signed

did.unittitle: Arnon, Judy and daughters Daniela and Gabriela

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four autograph letters, signed (one in English)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 19 autograph letters signed, to Leonor Fini, including two with drawings, mostly undated [1953-1963].

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph manuscript of "Leonor Fini. L'enchanteresse est assise…." 5 sheets written on verso; manuscript, 1 sheet on "Brown's Hotel, London" letterhead written verso and recto; manuscript, 1 sheet written on verso with text "Cette tour ne méritait pas d'être abattue..." and drawing of a tower, along with a photocopy; manuscript and corrected typescripts of recipes.

did.unittitle: Fini, Leonor to Jacques Audiberti, transcriptions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 45 typed transcriptions of Leonor Fini's letters to Audiberti from the 1950s; 1 letter dated January 25, 2004 from Josiane et Bernard Fournier to Richard Overstreet informing him they finished transcribing 45 letters of Fini to Audiberti.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three autograph letters, one postcard, signed, and the typescript of "Plus qu'on ne peut donner" by Claude Aveline

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two autograph letters, signed, one autograph poscard.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Seven autograph letters, signed from Hans Bellmer, including 2 jointly signed by Unica. One autograph letter, signed, from Unica Zürn.

scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, 4 sheets written on verso.

scopecontent.p: Eight autograph letters and one postcard, signed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, shares she is worried about Max Ernst

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three autograph letters, signed, two cards, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Over 20 autograph letters and postcards, typed letters, signed, with collaged elements and sketches, with one small black-and-white portrait photograph of Dario Cecchi. Shares updates on his art and exhibits and films, anecdotes about his days that are vaguely related to Fini or her interests (cat stories), his dreams, talks about mutual acquaintances, and inquires about Leonor's work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph postcard, signed, one card, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three autograph letters, signed, one autograph postcard, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed. He does not know Leonor personally and is asking recommendations on how to move to Paris. Sends salutations to "André."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, one typed letter, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed. With letters dated 1942-1947

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, one typed letter, signed. With letters dated 1942, 1945, 1948-1949

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approximately 28 autograph letters, signed, two autograph postcard, signed, one signed card, one photograph with signed note on back. With letters dated 1950, 1952-1954, 1956-1957

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Seven autograph letters, signed, five autograph postcards, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, three autograph postcards, signed. With letters dated 1981-1983, 1985-1986, 1988-1990

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One typescript of a poem, signed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two autograph letters, signed, one typed letter, signed, one card, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five autograph letters signed, to Leonor Fini. Paris, October 14, 1939; [Milly-la-Forêt], December 7, 1960; and Saint-Jean-Cap-Ferrat, December 20, 1960. autograph manuscript "Leonor Fini," signed (preface accompanying the catalog for Leonor Fini's exhibition held in Venice in September 1951 at the Palazzo Reale), with an autograph letter of transmittal signed, [Saint-Jean-Cap-Ferrat], July 4, 1951. Printed booklet, lecture given at the Vieux-Colombier, Paris, January 1932. In-8.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, one typed letter, signed, two autograph postcards, signed. Undated, except one postcards from 1953.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, several autograph postcards, signed. Also one autograph manuscript "Mélusine, charme du gris", 4 sheets written on recto. Text by Enrico Colmbotto Rosso translated by Stanislao Lepri. In French.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, one autograph postcard, signed. Only one letter from 1957, rest are undated

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, one autograph postcard, signed. One letter possibly from 1955-1977, rest are undated. 1 black-and-white photograph (22 x 16 cm) of Enrico, young, with autograph note on verso.
Includes letters to Kot, and two letters from Stanislao to Leonor Fini

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed. Includes letters from Leonardo Cremonini to Enrico, Enrico to Cremonini, Cremonini to Leonor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter from Jean Fraysse, signed, dated August 27, 1939. Another letter from unidentified correspondent, dated September 23, 1940, official letterhead: "Vice Présidence du Conseil / Information – Presse – Censure / Cabinet" responding to Fini's inquiry of Jean Fraysse's whereabouts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One gallery opening invitation with signed note

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 10 autograph letters, signed. One unfranked postcard signed by Funi, Leonor, and Alberto Magnelli.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed from Galerie 65 owned by Gilberte Duclaud

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One invitation card for a group exhibition at Galerie Delpire, Paris

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, one autograph postcard, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Eight autograph letters, signed and one autograph postcard, signed to Fini. One autograph letter, signed address to Cesare Pavani, undated. One autograph inscription signed "Jean Genet," to Leonor Fini on an old engraving depicting the Place de la Concorde. Original signed drawing with a dedication, in graphite, dedicated to Stanislao Lepri "souvenir, per la Lepre".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, typed letter, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts of letters. Generally, Leonor expresses her frustration and irritation with Norman Glass

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One typed letter, signed, two autograph postcards, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One notecard, one autograph letter, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approximately 15 autograph letters, signed, one autograph postcard, signed. Discusses his emotional state, travels, his paintings and his deep affection for Leonor. Mentions loved ones and friends (particularly Miriam and Nicola [Chiaromonte], Mario [Levi] and Anjo [Angelique Spitzer, Mario Levi's wife], "Bob" [composer Robert Mann].

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approximately 14 autograph letters, signed. Most letters written during his stay at the sanatorium in Helfaut, France, where he is treated for post-tuberculosis symptoms.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph letters, signed. Approximately 20 letters from 1972, one from [1973?], one from 1974. Discusses Leonor's health issues, mentions a trip to Nonza, a trip to visit "Bob" [Robert Mann]. One letter dated [1972?] October 23, addressed to Stanislao in which he is worried he might have offended Stanislao and hopes to regain his friendship. Writes from Rocca Sinibalda [his friend Robert Mann is staying at the castle of Rocca Sinibalda, north of Rome, under the patronage of Caresse Crosby], Bocca di Magra, and Paris.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, one typed letter, signed

did.unittitle: Guibbert, Jean Paul and Danièle Caligari

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, six autograph postcards, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, two autograph postcards, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In 1966, Leonor Fini had an affair with business man Paul Henri nicknamed "la Pieuvre" "le Cerf" (letters from March 1966 to May 1967).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, several typed letters, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, two typed letters, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, typed letter, signed, one autograph postcard, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, several typed letter, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five autograph postcards, signed, one holiday card, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five autograph letters, signed (two photocopied), two autograph postcards, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, five autograph postcards, signed, one signed notecard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, one signed name card

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, two typed letters, signed, two telegrams

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One typed letter, signed. Also draft letters from Fini to Izzo circa 1948.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three autograph letters, signed, one autograph postcard, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One telegram from Edward James to Sforzino Sforza; six original photographs (including 3 taken by Plutarco Gastelum, captioned on the verso by James in English and one in Italian, and by Gastelum himself in Spanish. 10 x 15 cm.); one illustrated autograph postcard signed "Edward," to Leonor Fini. Watercolored on the verso. 9 x 14 cm, in red ink; three autograph postcards signed by Plutarco Gastelum, one bearing the address of Leonor Fini, Rue Payenne, Paris. Cuernavaca, December 12, 1948. 9 x 14 cm; in Spanish, with two words in English.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Edward James, The Bones of My Hand. Oxford University Press, London, Humphrey Milford, 1938. In-4. Publisher's half-cloth binding under a dust jacket illustrated with a portrait of the author. A partially original edition, illustrated with a lithograph by Pavel Tchelitchew as a frontispiece. With an autograph inscription signed to Leonor Fini. With an original watercolor, captioned.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, one typed letter, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, one autograph postcard, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three autograph letters, signed, eight autograph postcards, signed. Mix of French, Italian, English, and German.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, two typed letters, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, one signed name card

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, one gallery invitation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two autograph letters, signed, two autograph postcards, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph postcard, signed, one signed name card

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, one typed letter, signed, two autograph postcards, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two autograph letters, signed, one autograph postcard, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approximately 18 autograph letters, signed and typed letter, signed, two autograph postcards, signed, one autograph note.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, several autograph postcards, signed, several signed cards, eight photograph prints

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, several typed letters, signed, one photograph print. Includes graphological analyses (M.B., Leonor, Reynold Arnould) and horoscopes by Leibl, in Italian. Also includes documents from other graphologists: 1934 March 6, graphological analysis of Leonor Fini, in French, signed "A. Blondel, Paris"; 1941 July 7, "Exercice de meditation pour Leonor Fini", signed Meredith Star, Monte Carlo, Monaco. 1944 March 19, graphological analysis, no name, in Italian.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, several typed letters, signed. Includes graphological analyses (includes Roger Peyrefitte) and horoscopes; 1 autograph letter addressed to Stanislao (1946). Also 1 graphology analysis of Leonor by Raymond Trillat (1949 November 7), in French.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, four typed letters, signed. Includes graphological analyses and horoscopes by Leibl.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, two typed letters, signed. Includes a graphology analysis of "Robert" by Raymond Trillat (1959 March 2).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, eight photgraph prints

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three autograph letters, signed, one autograph postcard, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two autograph letters, signed, one autograph postcard, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four autograph letters, signed, two autograph postcards, signed, one holiday card

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One signed holiday card, one invitation to a ball

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, one typed letter, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four autograph letters, signed and estimates for renovation of the monastery

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, one typed letter, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five autograph letters, signed, (November 24, 1948; December 16, 1948; June 24, 1956; October 27, 1956, undated 1956); three typed letters (August 11, 1956; September 1, 1956; September 27, 1956), signed, one signed notecard (September 27 1956)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two autograph letters signed. [Brussels], Saturday [circa 1948?] and Sunday. One autograph letter signed by Leonor Fini to René Magritte. Paris, February 14 [1948?]. Also 8 postcards depicting paintings by Magritte, with autograph titles and dates (1947) on recto, published by Gevaert.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, two autograph postcards, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three autograph letters, signed, two autograph postcards, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, two autograph postcards, signed

scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, two typed letters, signed, one signed notecard, one gallery invitation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, several typed letters, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, five photograph prints

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two autograph letters, signed, one signed poem

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, one autograph postcard, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, one typed letter, signed

scopecontent.p: Two autograph letters, signed, one autograph postcard, signed, one signed notecard

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two autograph letters, signed, one autograph postcard, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, six typed letters, signed, one autograph postcard, signed, two signed notecards, one signed photograph print

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph postcard, signed, one drawing/collage

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed and autograph postcard, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four autograph letters, signed, one autograph card, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, some letters from Kevin Torres, clipping, autograph postcard, signed, chiefly in French with some material in English

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One Fini drawing, several autograph letters, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, includes nine transparencies of set design, autograph postcard, signed, photo prints, clippings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, includes clipping "Attaques et autres troubles de vaisseaux sanguins", clipping "pour vous Messieurs", photo prints, letter to Overstreet from Sara Romilly, clipping "Juan Baptista Piñeiro, le poète le plus cher du monde", three photo prints of Fini works, one photo print of person with cat, clipping "Leonor Fini : peintures récentes", clipping "Leonor Fini Entre Ange et Démon"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, 56 x 76 cm photo print of S. Lepri drawing titled "Le Matin du Chasseur", clipping "Chez Leonor Fini, Le triomphe de la Belle Epoque", two Jan Lebenstein exhibition brochures

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, Fini original drawing of seated and standing figures, clipping "Leonor Fini Das Grobe Bilderbuc by Desch", drawings of cats being administered medicine, Fini exhibition brochure addressed to Overstreet, one photo print of Fini painting, one photo print of Fini

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph letter, signed, includes photo print of painting attached to the letter, correspondence also includes clippings glued together "Le Trafic de fausses lithos de Leonor Fini" and "Le marche trop florissant de l'oeuvre d'art pour tous", includes a journal article annotated by Leonor Fini, "Apostrophes de George Sand a Menie Grégoire l'émancipation de la femme", photo print of a canvas painted by leonor attached to autograph letters, signed, autograph letters, signed. Also includes exhibtion invitation, "Leonor Fini 20 November- 16 December 1976 - Galleria Forni in Amsterdam", includes exhbition invitation "Sultanes et Magiciennes des Mille et une Nuits", exhibition invitation "La Direction des Arts Graphiques Heliographia SA Lausanne vous présente ses meilleurs", clipping from "La Guilde du Livre Choix d'Auteurs Contemporains et Classiques", includes exhibition invitation "Autour de l'album Sultanes et Magiciennes des Mille et Une Nuits", photograph of "Le Serment Noir"; clipping "Contes Pout Petits et Grands Leonor fini Ecrivain Histoire de Chat" by Yves Florenne

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph letters, signed includes clipping of article "Revelli-Beaumont: ; le tango argentin", polaroid of artwork by Leonor, includes clipping "CET obscur objet du désir, de Luis Bunuel: La vierge aux bas noirs de Seville", includes 3 photo prints of a birthday cake commemorating Leonor's birthday, photographic contact sheet of Fini works, photo print of Fini work on canvas, clipping "La Mort de Jeanne", clippings about Don Carlo, copy of article "Surtout Les Puanteurs" by Leonor Fini

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph letters, signed, clipping "Un Voyage vers l'Asie ... " by Jean-Claude Guillebaud, includes exhibition invitation "La Galeries les Arts Plastiques Modernes, Leonor Fini Exposition du 22 Novembre au 20 December 1978", photocopy of clipping "Argus de la Presse: Deux expositions Leonor Fini A Paris" along with clipping "GUIDE: Leonor Fini: Roman-chat", typed letter from graphologist, clipping "Nos voeux a une grande dame: Leonor Fini", 5 black and white photo prints of cats, photo prints of Fini works, clipping from "Des Spectacles", clippings "Leonor fini: Une fête etrange et somptueuse", "les animaux ont-ils une conscience: Quatre pas dans l'univers d'un chat", and "Le beauté de Leonor fini", letter from the University of Tennessee College of Liberal arts asking Leonor to consider a faculty position

did.unittitle: Fini, Leonor to Richard Overstreet Drafts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two autograph letters, signed, two autograph notes with clipping

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pressed plants, three autograph letters, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two autograph letters, signed, one autograph postcard, signed, one typed letter

scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed with typed transcriptions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five autograph postcards from André to Leonor, one postcard from Leonor to André

Fini, Leonor to André Pieyre de Mandiargues (photocopies)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four autograph postcards, signed (photocopies). Folder included a note from Richard Overstreet indicating material had been loaned.

did.unittitle: Overstreet, Richard, note regarding loaned letters

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph note from Richard Overstreet regarding folder "1942, 1947, 1949". Inscribed "15-2-2016 - Lettres de Leonor Fini à APdeM: 1943 – 15 [letters], 1945 – 5 [letters], 1945 – 25 [letters] loaned to François Vatin working on book on Reynold Arnould (had an affair w/ Leonor in Monte Carlo"

did.unittitle: Fini, Leonor to André Pieyre de Mandiargues (photocopies)

__, Paola to André Pieyre de Mandiargues

scopecontent.p: One autograph postcard, signed. Addressed to APM "Hotel des Colonies, Montecarlo, Monaco"

did.unittitle: Unidentified to André Pieyre de Mandiargues

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two autograph letters, signed, typed transcription of correspondence from Leonor to Alberto Savinio

did.unittitle: Seibel, Castor "La fête dans le vide", typescript draft

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: From the André Guichard Collection. Sold at auction at Christie's, Paris, France, on March 21, 2024 (lot 226).

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: From the André Guichard Collection. Sold at auction at Christie's, Paris, France, on March 21, 2024 (lot 226).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, autograph postcards, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, autograph postcards, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, typed letter, signed

did.unittitle: Sforza, Sforzino, business file, G.H. Harms

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding business dealings including purchase and resale of film stock Gevaert

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, typed letter, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed, one autograph postcard, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, autograph postcards, signed. Some letters have drawings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Six autograph letters, two postcards, signed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three autograph letters, two autograph notecards, eight autograph postcards, one typed letter, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two autograph postcards, signed, one autograph letter, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, autograph postcards, signed, one Polaroid

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, one typed letter, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Several autograph letters, signed, one typed letter

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph postcard, signed, several autograph letters, signed

did.unittitle: Wang, Pao Ying (outgoing, Fini to Wang)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two typed letters, two autograph letters, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Approximately 13 autograph letters (including one to Corrado Cagli), signed, two autograph postcards, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One typed letter, signed, also one draft letter from Leonor Fini to Gabrielle Wittkop Ménardeau

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Five autograph postcards, signed, one photo slide

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter, signed. Likely Frédérique Genty Zaccar.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Seven autograph letters signed. Sent from Padua, Italy. One postcard from Milano on stationery of "Galleria Delia".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Eight autograph letters, signed, five autograph postcards, signed

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1986 July 8. 1 autograph letter, signed. Thanks Leonor for sending birthday card.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Other correspondence and collected materials contains draft letters from Leonor Fini to known and unknown recipients, letters from unidentified correspondents, groups of postcards from the 1930s to the 1990s that were kept in plastic binders and boxes, various notes and typescripts found in the correspondence, a small amount of press clippings, and fan mail.

did.unittitle: Draft letters to friends and acquaintances

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder includes an original watercolor drawing of a cat (16.5 cm x 10.5 cm) with the autograph note "Bonne Année, Leonor"; draft of letters, including to Federico Veneziani (vents grievances following their breakup), Stanislao Lepri, Reynold Arnould, Uguccione Sorbello. Also letters to unidentified recipients, one in French "Je maudissais ton atavisme protestant et ton affreuse mère qui t'a appris la dissimulation (Trillat aussi l'a constaté) et la faculté de mentir", another in Italian describing her social circle in Milan.

did.unittitle: Draft letters to friends and acquaintances

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 autograph letter, signed, 2 sheets written recto and verso. Italian artist friend of Leonor

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four autograph letters by same writer, signed, on letterhead "80 Boulevard de Courcelles, Paris XVII" and "148 Boulevard Haussman, Paris VIII (Bureau)". Likely a woman, possibly British socialite, her companion is "Pierre".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writer knows Anna Magnani and makes a reference to Sergio Gajardo.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One autograph letter with sketches, possibly from the 1960s: "Leonor Carissima, avant de partir, je voudrais te dire combien j'ai apprécié ce court séjour à Nonza, embelli par ta présence, hélas, gâché par la ménopause de Lepri". One autograph note on large notecard, dated September 11, 1973, sent from Geneva: "Gattora chérie, voici les premières Nouvelles du voyage : I) vendredi soir, le 14, je dois signer le contrat avec Monet et je suppose qu'un ou deux jours après, quand il te verra, tu signeras avec lui. Il était temps !"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 25 postcards. Including from Elena Vivante (1933), Bianca and Francesco Messina (circa 1934), Mino Maccari (1934, 1939), Achille Funi, Alberto Magnelli and Leonor Fini to Francisco Messina (1934?), Gregorio (1935), Malvina Fini to André Pieyre de Mandiargues (1935), Leonor Fini writing from Yugoslavia to André Pieyre de Mandiargues in Italy (September? 1937), Achille Funi writing from Tripoli, Lybia (1939), and Leonor Fini to [Leo] Longanesi (undated).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes postcards from Francesco Bellando, Peter Lindamood, "Hélène, "Luna", ["Pietra"?]. Also a postcard from Leonor Fini to "Rina" dated April 9, 1940, with her address as 87 Boulevard de la Plage, Arcachon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes postcards from [A.M.?] Gasser, Eugenio d'Ors.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes postcards from Elsa Morante, Alberto Moravia, Alida Valli, "Enrico".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes postcards from Liliane and Marcel Brion, Armand Lanoux, Meret [Oppenheim], Cesare [Pavani], "Chiessi", "Drew", "Olga", "Valentina". Also three blank postcards of landscapes and a black-and-white photograph depicting 4 men looking at a display case, including Cesare Pavani, and on the verso, an autograph note (including names "Enrico, Brice, David, Yvonne, Pauvert," and a phone number "WAGram 06-57")

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: 1 quadrille ruled page from small notebook inscribed with "H. Parisot", and "André Frédérique 50 bd de l'hopital"; autograph note "Danzas Domodossola / Prie de télegraphier…."; Scrap paper with autograph notes from Leonor "Rochas" "Mardi 8h M. Laure diner" "3 rue st. Paul infirmière / Brossard"; loose autograph note "Nuits Diamant chez Bonwit Teller / Elizabeth Arden, 691 Fifth Avenue, 2nd floor Mrs Gerry Winter…"; 1 loose sheet with autograph list in Leonor's handwriting "Lepre, malasser, Rochas, M. Louise…"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes one autograph, signed poem by Pierrette Micheloud "Moment avec Leonor Fini"; one typed poem with autograph corrections by Claude Saint Sever titled "le poisson d'or".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes one autograph, signed letter from Charles Zahar.
